title: Stage a remote configuration rollout using Fleet Management attributes

blocks.content:
Rolling out a new pipeline to your entire collector fleet at once is risky. A misconfigured pipeline can silently drop telemetry or break collectors in production before you can react.

Using the `env` attribute with regular expression matching, you can roll out a pipeline gradually — starting with dev, expanding to staging, then releasing to production in controlled stages. At any point, you can deactivate the pipeline with a single click to halt the rollout.

This guide assumes your collectors are already registered with Fleet Management. If you've completed the [Categorize a collector fleet](bundled:categorize-collector-fleet-lj) learning path, your `env` attributes are already set — the first section is just a quick verification check. If not, the steps below will get you set up.

blocks.title: Verify your collectors have env attributes

blocks.content: Open **Fleet Management**.
Click the **Inventory** tab.
Check the **Attributes** column. If your collectors already show `env` values, you're done with this section.
blocks.tooltip: The Attributes column shows all local and remote attributes currently assigned to each collector.

blocks.content: If your collectors are properly categorized, you can skip ahead to the next milestone. If any are missing `env` values, continue with the steps below to add the attribute.

whenTrue.content: Pop this guide out so you can keep it visible while you continue working.

blocks.title: Add the `env` attribute with the bulk edit tool

blocks.content: Select the collectors you want to tag by checking their checkboxes.
Click **Edit remote attributes** to open the bulk edit dialog.

In the dialog, click **Add remote attributes** to expand and complete the form:

1. In the **Key** field, type `env`.

2. In the **Value** field, type the environment name, for example `dev`.

3. Click **Apply changes** to save the attribute to all selected collectors.

Repeat the selection and assignment for each environment group, using values such as `staging`, `prod-eu`, and `prod-us` according to your deployment strategy. Once your collectors are tagged and ready, Fleet Management can use the `env` attribute to target specific environments when assigning pipelines.

blocks.title: Create the pipeline and target dev first

blocks.content: You'll create a configuration pipeline and use a regular expression matching attribute to deploy it only to your `dev` collectors.
Click the **Remote configuration** tab.
blocks.tooltip: The Remote configuration tab lists all configuration pipelines and lets you create, edit, activate, and deactivate them.

blocks.content: Click **Create configuration pipeline** to open the pipeline wizard.
Select the pipeline type and give it a name.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Card heading']:contains('Custom configuration') button:contains('Custom configuration')
steps.description: Select **Custom configuration** to write your own Alloy pipeline.
steps.tooltip: Use Custom configuration to define any Alloy pipeline from scratch.

steps.description: Give the pipeline a descriptive name, for example `linux_node_metrics_v2`. Include a version suffix so you can distinguish it from the pipeline it replaces.
steps.tooltip: A version suffix makes it easy to run the old and new pipeline side by side during a rollout.

steps.description: Click **Next** to open the pipeline editor.

blocks.content: Write or paste your Alloy configuration in the editor. The editor validates syntax as you type.
Validate your configuration and advance to the attribute matching step.

steps.description: Click **Test configuration pipeline** to validate your Alloy syntax before saving.
steps.tooltip: Testing catches syntax errors and component mismatches before the pipeline reaches any collector.

steps.description: Click **Next** to go to the **Assign configuration pipeline** step.

blocks.content: In the **Key** dropdown, select or type `env`.
blocks.tooltip: This is the attribute key you assigned to your collectors. Pipeline matching compares this key against each collector's attribute set.

blocks.content: Set the **Operator** to `=~` (regular expression) and type `dev` in the **Value** field. The collector list on the right updates in real time — only collectors with `env=dev` will appear.
blocks.tooltip: The =~ operator enables regex matching, so later you can expand dev to dev|staging without creating a new pipeline.

blocks.content: Click **Save** to activate the pipeline. Fleet Management immediately begins deploying it to your `dev` collectors on their next poll.
Your pipeline is now running in dev. Monitor your dev collectors in the Inventory tab and in your dashboards before expanding the rollout.

blocks.title: Expand the rollout to staging and production

blocks.content: You'll incrementally expand the pipeline's matching expression to include staging and then production environments.

Find your pipeline in the **Remote configuration** list and click the edit icon to reopen the wizard. Editing a pipeline takes you back into the wizard where you can update the matching attributes without changing the pipeline configuration content.

Navigate to the **Assign configuration pipeline** step. Update the matching attribute value to include staging:

- **Value:** `dev|staging`

The collector list updates to show collectors matching either `dev` or `staging`. Click **Save** to deploy the pipeline to staging.

Wait and verify that staging collectors are receiving the configuration and behaving as expected.

When staging looks healthy, edit the pipeline again and expand the regex to include your first production environment:

- **Value:** `dev|staging|prod-eu`

Save and monitor. Then repeat for your remaining production environments:

- **Value:** `dev|staging|prod-eu|prod-us`

Each expansion is a low-risk incremental step. If a problem appears at any stage, you can deactivate the pipeline before it reaches more of your fleet.
Your pipeline is now deployed across all environments. You've completed the staged rollout using the `env` attribute.
You'll learn how to immediately halt a rollout if a pipeline causes problems.
In the **Remote configuration** list, locate the **Active** toggle next to the pipeline you want to halt. Deactivating stops delivery immediately — no editing or attribute changes needed.

Click the **Active** toggle to deactivate the pipeline. Fleet Management stops sending this configuration to matched collectors on their next poll.

You don't need to change matching attributes or delete the pipeline — the deactivated pipeline stays in the list so you can reactivate it or use it as a reference when troubleshooting.

> **Tip:** Use Fleet Management's [**Pipelines history**](/docs/grafana-cloud/send-data/fleet-management/manage-fleet/pipelines/view-pipeline-history/) feature to review what changed and restore a previous version if needed.

You've learned how to deactivate a pipeline to halt a rollout instantly. Pairing this with a staged rollout strategy means problems can be caught in dev or staging before they reach production, and any production issues can be stopped with a single click.

You now have a repeatable strategy for safely rolling out configuration pipelines to your collector fleet. Use the `env` attribute and regex matching to control the blast radius of each change, and the **Active** toggle as your emergency stop.

To learn about related deployment patterns such as A/B testing, canary deployments, and blue-green environments, refer to [Streamline your workflows](/docs/grafana-cloud/send-data/fleet-management/streamline-workflows/).
